summary:
Claim: Selection bias arises when hospital-based data overestimate exposure probabilities due to systemic exclusion of less severe or underserved patient groups.  
Evidence: - Hospital records predominantly capture severe or acute cases, resulting in overrepresentation of exposed individuals. - Exclusion of outpatient or mildly symptomatic patients introduces non-random sampling, distorting true population-level exposure estimates.  
Answer: D
